================================Statement Details================================

Statement Coverage for instance /\csi_tb_top /dut/u_csi2_tx_top/u_regfile --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File tx_regfile.v
    23                                               module apb_csi2_tx_regfile #(
    24                                                   parameter integer APB_ADDR_WIDTH = 32
    25                                               ) (
    26                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    27                                                   // APB Clock and Reset
    28                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   input  wire                      PCLK,       // APB clock
    30                                                   input  wire                      PRESETn,    // APB reset, active-LOW
    31                                               
    32                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    33                                                   // APB Slave Interface
    34                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    35                                                   input  wire [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] PADDR,      // Byte address
    36                                                   input  wire                      PSEL,       // Peripheral select
    37                                                   input  wire                      PENABLE,    // ACCESS phase strobe
    38                                                   input  wire                      PWRITE,     // 1=Write 0=Read
    39                                                   input  wire [31:0]               PWDATA,     // Write data (32-bit bus)
    40                                                   input  wire [3:0]                PSTRB,      // Write byte strobes
    41                                               
    42                                                   output reg  [31:0]               PRDATA,     // Read data
    43                                                   output wire                      PREADY,     // Slave ready (no wait states)
    44                                                   output wire                      PSLVERR,    // Slave error
    45                                               
    46                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    47                                                   // Register outputs to CSI-2 TX Core
    48                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    49                                                   output reg                       tx_en,      // TX_CTRL[0] - TX Enable
    50                                                   output reg                       sw_rst,     // TX_CTRL[1] - SW Reset pulse
    51                                                   output reg                       scram_enb,  // TX_CFG[0]  - Scramble Enable
    52                                                   output reg                       crc_enb     // TX_CFG[1]  - CRC Enable
    53                                               );
    54                                               
    55                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    56                                                   // Local Parameters - Register Addresses
    57                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    58                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_TX_CTRL = 'h000;
    59                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_TX_CFG  = 'h004;
    60                                               
    61                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    62                                                   // Internal Wires - APB Phase & Address Decode
    63                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    64                                                   wire wr_en;
    65                                                   wire rd_en;
    66                                                   wire sel_tx_ctrl;
    67                                                   wire sel_tx_cfg;
    68                                                   wire addr_valid;
    69                                               
    70              1                        371         assign wr_en = PSEL & PENABLE &  PWRITE;
    71              1                        371         assign rd_en = PSEL & PENABLE & ~PWRITE;
    72                                               
    73              1                         84         assign sel_tx_ctrl = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_TX_CTRL[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    74              1                         84         assign sel_tx_cfg  = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_TX_CFG [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    75              1                         84         assign addr_valid  = sel_tx_ctrl | sel_tx_cfg;
    76                                               
    77                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    78                                                   // APB Response Signals
    79                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    80                                                   assign PREADY  = 1'b1;
    81              1                        372         assign PSLVERR = PSEL & PENABLE & ~addr_valid;
    82                                               
    83                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    84                                                   // Register Write Logic
    85                                                   //
    86                                                   // Priority (highest to lowest):
    87                                                   //   1. PRESETn LOW      - async reset clears all registers
    88                                                   //   2. SW_RST self-clear - takes priority over APB write in same cycle
    89                                                   //   3. APB write        - only fires when SW_RST is not self-clearing
    90                                                   //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    91              1                        536         always @(posedge PCLK or negedge PRESETn) begin
    92                                                       if (!PRESETn) begin
    93              1                         84                 tx_en     <= 1'b0;
    94              1                         84                 sw_rst    <= 1'b0;
    95              1                         84                 scram_enb <= 1'b0;
    96              1                         84                 crc_enb   <= 1'b0;
    97                                               
    98                                                       end else if (sw_rst) begin
    99              1                         41                 sw_rst <= 1'b0;
    100                                              
    101                                                      end else if (wr_en) begin
    102                                                          //----------------------------------------------------------------
    103                                                          // APB Write
    104                                                          //----------------------------------------------------------------
    105                                              
    106                                                          // TX_CTRL @ 0x000
    107                                                          if (sel_tx_ctrl && PSTRB[0]) begin
    108             1                         82                     tx_en  <= PWDATA[0];    // [0] TX_EN
    109             1                         82                     sw_rst <= PWDATA[1];    // [1] SW_RST
    110                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    111                                                          end
    112                                              
    113                                                          // TX_CFG @ 0x004
    114                                                          if (sel_tx_cfg && PSTRB[0]) begin
    115             1                         41                     scram_enb <= PWDATA[0]; // [0] SCRAM_ENB
    116             1                         41                     crc_enb   <= PWDATA[1]; // [1] CRC_ENB
    117                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    118                                                          end
    119                                              
    120                                                      end
    121                                                  end
    122                                              
    123                                                  //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    124                                                  // APB Read
    125                                                  //----------------------------------------------------------------------
    126             1                        280         always @(*) begin
    127             1                        280             PRDATA = 32'h0000_0000;
    128                                              
    129                                                      if (rd_en) begin

  File async_fifo_ppi_tx.v
    18                                               module async_fifo_ppi_tx #(  
    19                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    20                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    21                                               )(
    22                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source)
    24                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,
    26                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n, 
    27                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,
    28                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,
    29                                                   output wire              o_full,
    30                                               
    31                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination)
    33                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,
    35                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n, 
    36                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,
    37                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,
    38                                                   output wire              o_empty
    39                                               );
    40                                               
    41                                                   // =========================================================================
    42                                                   // Local Parameters & Internal Signals
    43                                                   // =========================================================================
    44                                                   // Address width calculation based on parameterized FIFO depth
    45                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    46                                               
    47                                                   // Internal Memory Array (Dual-Port RAM configuration)
    48                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointers: ADDR_WIDTH+1 is used for Full/Empty wrap-around detection
    51                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    52                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    53                                                   
    54                                                   // Synchronizer Flip-Flops (2-stage synchronizers for CDC safety)
    55                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    56                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    57                                               
    58                                                   // Combinational next-state pointers for faster evaluation
    59              1                    6279377         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    60              1                    6279376         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    61                                                   
    62                                                   // Loop variable for DFT-compliant memory reset
    63                                                   integer j;
    64                                               
    65                                                   // =========================================================================
    66                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    67                                                   // Description: Handles pointer incrementing and Binary-to-Gray conversion.
    68                                                   //              Binary pointers are used for memory addressing, while Gray 
    69                                                   //              pointers are used for safe clock domain crossing.
    70                                                   // =========================================================================
    71              1                   10678666         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        152                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    74              1                        152                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0; 
    75                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    76              1                    6279355                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    77                                                           // Binary to Gray conversion: XOR shifted value with original
    78              1                    6279355                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    79                                                       end
    80                                                   end
    81                                               
    82                                                   // =========================================================================
    83                                                   // 2. Memory Write Operation (DFT-Compliant)
    84                                                   // Description: Writes data to the memory array. Incorporates a full reset 
    85                                                   //              loop to synthesize into standard D-Flip-Flops with reset 
    86                                                   //              pins, ensuring compatibility with DFT scan chains.
    87                                                   // =========================================================================
    88              1                   10678666         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    89                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    90              1                        152                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    90              2                       2432     
    91              1                       2432                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    92                                                           end
    93                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    94              1                    6279355                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    95                                                       end
    96                                                   end
    97                                               
    98                                                   // =========================================================================
    99                                                   // 3. Read Domain Logic
    100                                                  // Description: Manages the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    101                                                  // =========================================================================
    102             1                   13199282         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    103                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    104             1                         82                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    105             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    106                                                      end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    107             1                    6279354                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    108                                                          // Binary to Gray conversion
    109             1                    6279354                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    110                                                      end
    111                                                  end
    112                                                  
    113                                                  // Continuous assignment for data output, gated by the 'o_empty' flag
    114                                                  // to prevent reading garbage/stale data.
    115             1                   14143448         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 4. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    119                                                  // Description: Synchronizes Gray-coded pointers into the opposite domain
    120                                                  //              using standard 2-FF synchronizers to mitigate metastability.
    121                                                  // =========================================================================
    122                                                  
    123                                                  // Synchronize Read Pointer into Write Clock Domain
    124             1                   13199386         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    125                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    126             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    127             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    128                                                      end else begin
    129             1                   13199234                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    130             1                   13199234                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    131                                                      end
    132                                                  end
    133                                              
    134                                                  // Synchronize Write Pointer into Read Clock Domain
    135             1                   13199275         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    136                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    137             1                         94                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    138             1                         94                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    139                                                      end else begin
    140             1                   13199181                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    141             1                   13199181                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    142                                                      end
    143                                                  end
    144                                              
    145                                                  // =========================================================================
    146                                                  // 5. Status Flag Generation
    147                                                  // Description: 
    148                                                  //   - o_full : True if write pointer matches synchronized read pointer, 
    149                                                  //              accounting for the wrap-around bit in the Gray domain.
    150                                                  //   - o_empty: True if read pointer matches synchronized write pointer.
    151                                                  // =========================================================================
    152             1                    9746511         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    153             1                    9740495         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    115             1                   12254924         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 4. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    119                                                  // Description: Synchronizes Gray-coded pointers into the opposite domain
    120                                                  //              using standard 2-FF synchronizers to mitigate metastability.
    121                                                  // =========================================================================
    122                                                  
    123                                                  // Synchronize Read Pointer into Write Clock Domain
    124             1                   11315482         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    125                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    126             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    127             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    128                                                      end else begin
    129             1                   11315330                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    130             1                   11315330                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    131                                                      end
    132                                                  end
    133                                              
    134                                                  // Synchronize Write Pointer into Read Clock Domain
    135             1                   11315375         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    136                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    137             1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    138             1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    139                                                      end else begin
    140             1                   11315293                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    141             1                   11315293                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    142                                                      end
    143                                                  end
    144                                              
    145                                                  // =========================================================================
    146                                                  // 5. Status Flag Generation
    147                                                  // Description: 
    148                                                  //   - o_full : True if write pointer matches synchronized read pointer, 
    149                                                  //              accounting for the wrap-around bit in the Gray domain.
    150                                                  //   - o_empty: True if read pointer matches synchronized write pointer.
    151                                                  // =========================================================================
    152             1                    7857794         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    153             1                    7856586         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    18                                               module async_fifo_ppi_tx #(  
    19                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    20                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    21                                               )(
    22                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source)
    24                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,
    26                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n, 
    27                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,
    28                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,
    29                                                   output wire              o_full,
    30                                               
    31                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination)
    33                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,
    35                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n, 
    36                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,
    37                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,
    38                                                   output wire              o_empty
    39                                               );
    40                                               
    41                                                   // =========================================================================
    42                                                   // Local Parameters & Internal Signals
    43                                                   // =========================================================================
    44                                                   // Address width calculation based on parameterized FIFO depth
    45                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    46                                               
    47                                                   // Internal Memory Array (Dual-Port RAM configuration)
    48                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointers: ADDR_WIDTH+1 is used for Full/Empty wrap-around detection
    51                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    52                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    53                                                   
    54                                                   // Synchronizer Flip-Flops (2-stage synchronizers for CDC safety)
    55                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    56                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    57                                               
    58                                                   // Combinational next-state pointers for faster evaluation
    59              1                    2517173         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    60              1                    2517172         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    61                                                   
    62                                                   // Loop variable for DFT-compliant memory reset
    63                                                   integer j;
    64                                               
    65                                                   // =========================================================================
    66                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    67                                                   // Description: Handles pointer incrementing and Binary-to-Gray conversion.
    68                                                   //              Binary pointers are used for memory addressing, while Gray 
    69                                                   //              pointers are used for safe clock domain crossing.
    70                                                   // =========================================================================
    71              1                    5034554         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        152                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    74              1                        152                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0; 
    75                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    76              1                    2517163                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    77                                                           // Binary to Gray conversion: XOR shifted value with original
    78              1                    2517163                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    79                                                       end
    80                                                   end
    81                                               
    82                                                   // =========================================================================
    83                                                   // 2. Memory Write Operation (DFT-Compliant)
    84                                                   // Description: Writes data to the memory array. Incorporates a full reset 
    85                                                   //              loop to synthesize into standard D-Flip-Flops with reset 
    86                                                   //              pins, ensuring compatibility with DFT scan chains.
    87                                                   // =========================================================================
    88              1                    5034554         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    89                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    90              1                        152                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    90              2                       2432     
    91              1                       2432                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    92                                                           end
    93                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    94              1                    2517163                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    95                                                       end
    96                                                   end
    97                                               
    98                                                   // =========================================================================
    99                                                   // 3. Read Domain Logic
    100                                                  // Description: Manages the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    101                                                  // =========================================================================
    102             1                    7551610         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    103                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    104             1                         82                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    105             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    106                                                      end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    107             1                    2517162                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    108                                                          // Binary to Gray conversion
    109             1                    2517162                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    110                                                      end
    111                                                  end
    112                                                  
    113                                                  // Continuous assignment for data output, gated by the 'o_empty' flag
    114                                                  // to prevent reading garbage/stale data.
    115             1                    7553136         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 4. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    119                                                  // Description: Synchronizes Gray-coded pointers into the opposite domain
    120                                                  //              using standard 2-FF synchronizers to mitigate metastability.
    121                                                  // =========================================================================
    122                                                  
    123                                                  // Synchronize Read Pointer into Write Clock Domain
    124             1                    7551714         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    125                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    126             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    127             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    128                                                      end else begin
    129             1                    7551562                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    130             1                    7551562                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    131                                                      end
    132                                                  end
    133                                              
    134                                                  // Synchronize Write Pointer into Read Clock Domain
    135             1                    7551595         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    136                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    137             1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    138             1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    139                                                      end else begin
    140             1                    7551513                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    141             1                    7551513                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    142                                                      end
    143                                                  end
    144                                              
    145                                                  // =========================================================================
    146                                                  // 5. Status Flag Generation
    147                                                  // Description: 
    148                                                  //   - o_full : True if write pointer matches synchronized read pointer, 
    149                                                  //              accounting for the wrap-around bit in the Gray domain.
    150                                                  //   - o_empty: True if read pointer matches synchronized write pointer.
    151                                                  // =========================================================================
    152             1                    5034335         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    153             1                    5034335         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    54                                                   // Synchronizer Flip-Flops (2-stage synchronizers for CDC safety)

    56                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    57                                               
    58                                                   // Combinational next-state pointers for faster evaluation
    59              1                    2517173         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    60              1                    2517172         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    61                                                   
    62                                                   // Loop variable for DFT-compliant memory reset
    63                                                   integer j;
    64                                               
    65                                                   // =========================================================================
    66                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    67                                                   // Description: Handles pointer incrementing and Binary-to-Gray conversion.
    68                                                   //              Binary pointers are used for memory addressing, while Gray 
    69                                                   //              pointers are used for safe clock domain crossing.
    70                                                   // =========================================================================
    71              1                    5034554         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                        152                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    74              1                        152                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0; 
    75                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    76              1                    2517163                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    77                                                           // Binary to Gray conversion: XOR shifted value with original
    78              1                    2517163                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    79                                                       end
    80                                                   end
    81                                               
    82                                                   // =========================================================================
    83                                                   // 2. Memory Write Operation (DFT-Compliant)
    84                                                   // Description: Writes data to the memory array. Incorporates a full reset 
    85                                                   //              loop to synthesize into standard D-Flip-Flops with reset 
    86                                                   //              pins, ensuring compatibility with DFT scan chains.
    87                                                   // =========================================================================
    88              1                    5034554         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    89                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    90              1                        152                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    90              2                       2432     
    91              1                       2432                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    92                                                           end
    93                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    94              1                    2517163                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    95                                                       end
    96                                                   end
    97                                               
    98                                                   // =========================================================================
    99                                                   // 3. Read Domain Logic
    100                                                  // Description: Manages the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    101                                                  // =========================================================================
    102             1                    7551610         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    103                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    104             1                         82                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    105             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    106                                                      end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    107             1                    2517162                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    108                                                          // Binary to Gray conversion
    109             1                    2517162                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    110                                                      end
    111                                                  end
    112                                                  
    113                                                  // Continuous assignment for data output, gated by the 'o_empty' flag
    114                                                  // to prevent reading garbage/stale data.
    115             1                    7553133         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    116                                              
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  // 4. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    119                                                  // Description: Synchronizes Gray-coded pointers into the opposite domain
    120                                                  //              using standard 2-FF synchronizers to mitigate metastability.
    121                                                  // =========================================================================
    122                                                  
    123                                                  // Synchronize Read Pointer into Write Clock Domain
    124             1                    7551714         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    125                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    126             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    127             1                        152                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    128                                                      end else begin
    129             1                    7551562                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    130             1                    7551562                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    131                                                      end
    132                                                  end
    133                                              
    134                                                  // Synchronize Write Pointer into Read Clock Domain
    135             1                    7551595         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    136                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    137             1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    138             1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    139                                                      end else begin
    140             1                    7551513                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    141             1                    7551513                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    142                                                      end
    143                                                  end
    144                                              
    145                                                  // =========================================================================
    146                                                  // 5. Status Flag Generation
    147                                                  // Description: 
    148                                                  //   - o_full : True if write pointer matches synchronized read pointer, 
    149                                                  //              accounting for the wrap-around bit in the Gray domain.
    150                                                  //   - o_empty: True if read pointer matches synchronized write pointer.
    151                                                  // =========================================================================
    152             1                    5034335         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    153             1                    5034335         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    14                                               module ppi_tx (
    15                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16                                                   // System Clocks and Resets
    17                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    18                                                   input  wire        i_clk_sys,    
    19                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n_sys,
    20                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n_tx,
    21                                                   input  wire [2:0]  i_active_lanes,
    22                                                   
    23                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                                   // Internal Interface (From Lane Distributor)
    25                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    26                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_lane0_data,
    27                                                   input  wire        i_lane0_vld,  
    28                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_lane1_data,
    29                                                   input  wire        i_lane1_vld,

    34                                                   
    35                                                   input  wire        i_req_hs,     
    36                                                   
    37                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38                                                   // MIPI D-PHY PPI High-Speed Control & Data Interface
    39                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    40                                                   output reg         o_TxClkRequestHS,
    41                                                   input  wire        i_TxClkReadyHS,
    42                                                   input  wire        i_TxByteClkHS,     
    43                                                   
    44                                                   output wire [3:0]  o_TxRequestHS,
    45                                                   input  wire [3:0]  i_TxReadyHS,
    46                                                   
    47                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_lane0_data_hs, 
    48                                                   output wire        o_lane0_valid_hs,
    49                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_lane1_data_hs, 
    50                                                   output wire        o_lane1_valid_hs,
    51                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_lane2_data_hs, 
    52                                                   output wire        o_lane2_valid_hs,
    53                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_lane3_data_hs, 
    54                                                   output wire        o_lane3_valid_hs,
    55                                               
    56                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    57                                                   // MIPI D-PHY PPI Control & Low-Power (ULPM) Signals
    58                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    59                                                   input  wire        i_Shutdown,
    60                                                   input  wire        i_TxClkEsc,
    61                                                   output wire        o_TxUlpmClk,
    62                                                   output wire [3:0]  o_TxRequestEsc,
    63                                                   output wire [3:0]  o_TxUlpmEsc
    64                                               );
    65                                               
    66                                                   // =========================================================================
    67                                                   // 1. High-Speed Clock Request & Keep-Alive Logic
    68                                                   // Concept: Ensures the HS clock remains stable and active for at least 
    69                                                   //          TCLK-POST time after the packet request drops, preventing jitter 
    70                                                   //          and ensuring clean packet termination at the D-PHY level.
    71                                                   // Note:    Targeting 320 Mbps (Lane) -> clk_sys = 160 MHz (6.25ns).
    72                                                   //          TCLK-POST required ~222.5ns. 40 cycles * 6.25ns = 250ns (Safe margin).
    73                                                   // =========================================================================
    74                                                   reg [5:0] keep_alive_cnt; 
    75              1                     176677         always @(posedge i_clk_sys or negedge i_rst_n_sys) begin
    76                                                       if (!i_rst_n_sys) begin
    77              1                        152                 keep_alive_cnt   <= 6'd0;
    78              1                        152                 o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b0;
    79                                                       end else begin
    80                                                           if (i_req_hs) begin
    81              1                       8150                     o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b1;
    82              1                       8150                     keep_alive_cnt   <= 6'd40; // Modified from 31 to 40 for 320 Mbps Specs
    83                                                           end else if (keep_alive_cnt > 0) begin
    84              1                     160297                     o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b1;
    85              1                     160297                     keep_alive_cnt   <= keep_alive_cnt - 1;
    86                                                           end else begin
    87              1                       8078                     o_TxClkRequestHS <= 1'b0;
    88                                                           end
    89                                                       end
    90                                                   end
    91                                               
    92                                                   // =========================================================================
    93                                                   // 2. Escape Clock Domain Sink (Synthesis Workaround)
    94                                                   // Concept: Implements a dummy flip-flop to act as a sequential sink for 
    95                                                   //          'i_TxClkEsc', satisfying EDA tools (e.g., SpyGlass PCLK04) 
    96                                                   //          while maintaining a strict ASIC-compliant synthesis path.
    97                                                   // =========================================================================
    98                                                   reg r_dummy_esc_ff; 
    99              1                    ***0***         always @(posedge i_TxClkEsc) begin
    100             1                    ***0***             r_dummy_esc_ff <= ~r_dummy_esc_ff; 
    101                                                  end
    102                                              
    103                                                  // =========================================================================
    104                                                  // 3. CIL Power Management Logic (ULPM Control)
    105                                                  // Concept: Dynamically triggers Ultra Low Power Mode (ULPM) when the 
    106                                                  //          controller is idle and not requesting High-Speed mode.
    107                                                  // =========================================================================
    108             1                      16175         wire w_is_idle = ~(o_TxClkRequestHS | i_req_hs);
    109             1                       8006         assign o_TxUlpmClk    = w_is_idle & ~i_Shutdown;
    110             1                       8006         assign o_TxRequestEsc = {4{w_is_idle & ~i_Shutdown}};
    111             1                       8006         assign o_TxUlpmEsc    = {4{w_is_idle & ~i_Shutdown}};
    112                                              
    113                                                  // =========================================================================
    114                                                  // 4. Data Path & Clock Domain Crossing (CDC)
    115                                                  // Architecture: 4 Independent Asynchronous FIFOs to safely transport data 
    116                                                  //               from the System Clock domain to the D-PHY HS Clock domain.
    117                                                  // =========================================================================
    118                                                  wire w_empty_0, w_empty_1, w_empty_2, w_empty_3;
    119                                                  wire [7:0] w_raw_data_0, w_raw_data_1, w_raw_data_2, w_raw_data_3;
    120                                                  wire w_fifo_full_0, w_fifo_full_1, w_fifo_full_2, w_fifo_full_3;
    121                                              
    122                                                  // Read enables gated by PPI Ready signals
    123             1                   13839612         wire w_rd_en_0 = o_TxRequestHS[0] & i_TxReadyHS[0];
    124             1                   13834796         wire w_rd_en_1 = o_TxRequestHS[1] & i_TxReadyHS[1];
    125             1                   10068652         wire w_rd_en_2 = o_TxRequestHS[2] & i_TxReadyHS[2];
    126             1                   10068652         wire w_rd_en_3 = o_TxRequestHS[3] & i_TxReadyHS[3];
    127                                              
    128                                                  async_fifo_ppi_tx #(.WIDTH(8), .DEPTH(16)) fifo0 (

    159                                              
    160                                                  // =========================================================================
    161                                                  // 5. Synthesis Optimization & Structural Sink
    162                                                  // Concept: Uses an AND-reduction with 1'b0 to create a safe structural 
    163                                                  //          sink for unused internal status signals, ensuring the RTL 
    164                                                  //          remains clean of floating nets during gate-level synthesis.
    165                                                  // =========================================================================
    166             1                         39         wire w_dummy_sink = &{1'b0, i_active_lanes, w_fifo_full_0, w_fifo_full_1, w_fifo_full_2, w_fifo_full_3, r_dummy_esc_ff, i_Shutdown};
    167                                              
    168                                                  // The dummy sink is logically OR'ed with o_TxRequestHS[0] (X | 0 = X)
    169             1                    6927812         assign o_TxRequestHS[0] = (i_TxClkReadyHS & ~w_empty_0) | w_dummy_sink;

    48                                                   // Description: Prevents recovery metastability by ensuring resets are

Statement Coverage for instance /\csi_tb_top /dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_regfile --

  File rx_regfile.v
    30                                               module apb_csi2_rx_regfile #(
    31                                                   parameter integer APB_ADDR_WIDTH = 32
    32                                               ) (
    33                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   // APB Clock and Reset
    35                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36                                                   input  wire                      PCLK,           // APB clock (rising-edge)
    37                                                   input  wire                      PRESETn,        // APB reset, active-LOW
    38                                               
    39                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40                                                   // APB Slave Interface
    41                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42                                                   input  wire [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] PADDR,          // Byte address
    43                                                   input  wire                      PSEL,           // Peripheral select
    44                                                   input  wire                      PENABLE,        // ACCESS phase strobe
    45                                                   input  wire                      PWRITE,         // 1=Write  0=Read
    46                                                   input  wire [31:0]               PWDATA,         // Write data
    47                                                   input  wire [3:0]                PSTRB,          // Write byte strobes
    48                                               
    49                                                   output reg  [31:0]               PRDATA,         // Read data
    50                                                   output wire                      PREADY,         // Slave ready 
    51                                                   output wire                      PSLVERR,        // Slave error
    52                                               
    53                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    54                                                   // Register outputs to CSI-2 RX 
    55                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    56                                                   output reg                       rx_en,          // RX_CTRL[0] - RX Enable
    57                                                   output reg                       sw_rst,         // RX_CTRL[1] - SW Reset pulse
    58                                                   output reg                       rx_scram_enb,   // RX_CFG[0]  - Scramble Enable
    59                                                   output reg                       rx_crc_enb,     // RX_CFG[1]  - CRC Enable
    60                                               
    61                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62                                                   // error inputs from CSI-2 RX 
    63                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64                                                   input  wire                      hw_crc_err,     // RX_ERR[0] - CRC error 
    65                                                   input  wire                      hw_ecc_err,     // RX_ERR[1] - ECC error 
    66                                                   input  wire                      hw_cecc_err     // RX_ERR[2] - Corrected ECC 
    67                                               );
    68                                               
    69                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70                                                   // Local Parameters - Register Addresses 
    71                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_RX_CTRL = 'h000;
    73                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_RX_CFG  = 'h004;
    74                                                   localparam [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] ADDR_RX_ERR  = 'h00C;
    75                                               
    76                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77                                                   // Internal Wires
    78                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    79                                                   wire wr_en;
    80                                                   wire rd_en;
    81                                                   wire sel_rx_ctrl;
    82                                                   wire sel_rx_cfg;
    83                                                   wire sel_rx_err;
    84                                                   wire addr_valid;
    85                                               
    86              1                        371         assign wr_en = PSEL & PENABLE &  PWRITE;
    87              1                        371         assign rd_en = PSEL & PENABLE & ~PWRITE;
    88                                               
    89              1                         84         assign sel_rx_ctrl = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_RX_CTRL[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    90              1                         84         assign sel_rx_cfg  = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_RX_CFG [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    91              1                         84         assign sel_rx_err  = (PADDR[APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2] == ADDR_RX_ERR [APB_ADDR_WIDTH-1:2]);
    92              1                         84         assign addr_valid  = sel_rx_ctrl | sel_rx_cfg | sel_rx_err;
    93                                               
    94                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95                                                   // APB Response Signals
    96                                                   //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    97                                                   assign PREADY  = 1'b1;
    98              1                        372         assign PSLVERR = PSEL & PENABLE & ~addr_valid;
    99                                               
    100                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    101                                                  // Sticky error registers
    102                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    103                                                  reg crc_err_sticky;
    104                                                  reg ecc_err_sticky;
    105                                                  reg cecc_err_sticky;
    106                                              
    107                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    108                                                  // R/W Register Write Logic
    109                                                  //
    110                                                  // Priority (highest to lowest):
    111                                                  //   1. PRESETn LOW       - async reset clears all R/W registers
    112                                                  //   2. SW_RST self-clear - takes priority over APB write in same cycle
    113                                                  //   3. APB write         - only fires when SW_RST is not self-clearing
    114                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    115             1                        536         always @(posedge PCLK or negedge PRESETn) begin
    116                                                      if (!PRESETn) begin
    117             1                         84                 rx_en        <= 1'b0;
    118             1                         84                 sw_rst       <= 1'b0;
    119             1                         84                 rx_scram_enb <= 1'b0;
    120             1                         84                 rx_crc_enb   <= 1'b0;
    121                                              
    122                                                      end else if (sw_rst) begin
    123                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    124                                                          // SW_RST self-clear: highest priority after reset.
    125                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    126             1                         41                 sw_rst <= 1'b0;
    127                                              
    128                                                      end else if (wr_en) begin
    129                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    130                                                          // APB Write: only reached when sw_rst is not self-clearing.
    131                                                          //------------------------------------------------------------------
    132                                              
    133                                                          // RX_CTRL @ 0x000
    134                                                          if (sel_rx_ctrl && PSTRB[0]) begin
    135             1                         82                     rx_en  <= PWDATA[0];    // [0] RX_EN
    136             1                         82                     sw_rst <= PWDATA[1];    // [1] SW_RST
    137                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    138                                                          end
    139                                              
    140                                                          // RX_CFG @ 0x004
    141                                                          if (sel_rx_cfg && PSTRB[0]) begin
    142             1                         41                     rx_scram_enb <= PWDATA[0]; // [0] RX_SCRAM_ENB
    143             1                         41                     rx_crc_enb   <= PWDATA[1]; // [1] RX_CRC_ENB
    144                                                              // [31:2] reserved
    145                                                          end
    146                                              
    147                                                      end
    148                                                  end
    149                                              
    150                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    151                                                  // RX_ERR Sticky Bit Logic
    152                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    153             1                        126         always @(posedge PCLK or negedge PRESETn) begin
    154                                                      if (!PRESETn) begin
    155             1                         84                 crc_err_sticky  <= 1'b0;
    156             1                         84                 ecc_err_sticky  <= 1'b0;
    157             1                         84                 cecc_err_sticky <= 1'b0;
    158                                                      end else begin
    159             1                    ***0***                 if (hw_crc_err)  crc_err_sticky  <= 1'b1;
    160             1                    ***0***                 if (hw_ecc_err)  ecc_err_sticky  <= 1'b1;
    161             1                    ***0***                 if (hw_cecc_err) cecc_err_sticky <= 1'b1;
    162                                                      end
    163                                                  end
    164                                              
    165                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    166                                                  // Register Read Logic
    167                                                  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
    168             1                        280         always @(*) begin
    169             1                        280             PRDATA = 32'h0000_0000;
    170                                              
    171                                                      if (rd_en) begin
    172                                                          case (1'b1)
    173             1                    ***0***                     sel_rx_ctrl : PRDATA = {{30{1'b0}}, sw_rst,          rx_en        };
    174             1                    ***0***                     sel_rx_cfg  : PRDATA = {{30{1'b0}}, rx_crc_enb,      rx_scram_enb };

    176                                                                                                  ecc_err_sticky,
