  File async_fifo_ppi_rx.v
    15                                               module async_fifo_ppi_rx #(
    16                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    17                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    18                                               )(
    19                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source: D-PHY Clock Domain)
    21                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    22                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,     // D-PHY Byte Clock
    23                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to wr_clk
    24                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,      // Write enable
    25                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,        // Data input from PPI
    26                                                   output wire              o_full,       // FIFO full flag
    27                                               
    28                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination: System Clock Domain)
    30                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,     // Internal System Clock
    32                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to rd_clk
    33                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,      // Read enable from Lane Merger
    34                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,       // Data output to Lane Merger
    35                                                   output wire              o_empty       // FIFO empty flag
    36                                               );
    37                                               
    38                                                   // Address width calculation
    39                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    40                                               
    41                                                   // Internal Memory and Pointer Registers
    42                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    43                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    44                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    45                                                   
    46                                                   // Cross-Domain Synchronizers (2-Stage / Double-Flop)
    47                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    48                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointer increments
    51              1                    6279376         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    52              1                    6279375         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    53                                                   integer j;
    54                                                   // =========================================================================
    55                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    56                                                   // Description: Updates the write pointer and its Gray-coded version.
    57                                                   //              Writes are only performed when 'i_wr_en' is active and FIFO 
    58                                                   //              is not full. Binary pointer is used for memory addressing.
    59                                                   // =========================================================================
    60              1                    9739379         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    61                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    62              1                         82                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    63              1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0;
    64                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    65              1                    6279354                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    66              1                    6279354                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    67                                                       end
    68                                                   end
    69                                               
    70                                                   // Synthesis of the Memory Array (Register-based)
    71              1                    9739379         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                         82                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    73              2                       1312     
    74              1                       1312                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    75                                                           end
    76                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    77              1                    6279354                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // =========================================================================
    82                                                   // 2. Read Domain Logic
    83                                                   // Description: Updates the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    84                                                   //              Ensures safe data output only when FIFO is not empty.
    85                                                   //              Gray pointers are used to safely pass the pointer across domains.
    86                                                   // =========================================================================
    87              1                   10710658         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    88                                                       if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    89              1                         84                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    90              1                         84                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    91                                                       end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    92              1                    6279354                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    93              1                    6279354                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    94                                                       end
    95                                                   end
    96                                                   
    97                                                   // Data Output Assignment (zeroed if empty to prevent data leakage)
    98              1                    7295653         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    99                                               
    100                                                  // =========================================================================
    101                                                  // 3. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    102                                                  // Description: Synchronizes pointers between the two asynchronous domains.
    103                                                  //              Gray coding ensures only one bit changes per transition, 
    104                                                  //              which prevents incorrect intermediate values during sampling.
    105                                                  // =========================================================================
    106                                                  
    107                                                  // Cross Read Pointer into Write Domain
    108             1                   13198102         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    109                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    110             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    111             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    112                                                      end else begin
    113             1                   13198020                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    114             1                   13198020                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    115                                                      end
    116                                                  end
    117                                              
    118                                                  // Cross Write Pointer into Read Domain
    119             1                   13199284         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    120                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    121             1                         84                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    122             1                         84                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    123                                                      end else begin
    124             1                   13199200                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    125             1                   13199200                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    126                                                      end
    127                                                  end
    128                                              
    129                                                  // =========================================================================
    130                                                  // 4. Status Flag Generation
    131                                                  // Description: Compares Gray-coded pointers to determine FIFO status.
    132                                                  //              - o_empty: True if read pointer equals synchronized write pointer.
    133                                                  //              - o_full : True if write pointer MSBs are inverted relative to 
    134                                                  //                         synchronized read pointer (indicating wrap-around).
    135                                                  // =========================================================================
    136             1                    9752473         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    137             1                    9746485         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    64                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    65              1                    4397858                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    66              1                    4397858                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    67                                                       end
    68                                                   end
    69                                               
    70                                                   // Synthesis of the Memory Array (Register-based)
    71              1                    7856679         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                         82                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    73              2                       1312     
    74              1                       1312                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    75                                                           end
    76                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    77              1                    4397858                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // =========================================================================
    82                                                   // 2. Read Domain Logic
    83                                                   // Description: Updates the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    84                                                   //              Ensures safe data output only when FIFO is not empty.
    85                                                   //              Gray pointers are used to safely pass the pointer across domains.
    86                                                   // =========================================================================
    87              1                    8826762         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    88                                                       if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin

    90              1                         84                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    91                                                       end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    92              1                    4397858                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    93              1                    4397858                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    94                                                       end
    95                                                   end
    96                                                   
    97                                                   // Data Output Assignment (zeroed if empty to prevent data leakage)
    98              1                    5402075         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    99                                               
    100                                                  // =========================================================================
    101                                                  // 3. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    102                                                  // Description: Synchronizes pointers between the two asynchronous domains.
    103                                                  //              Gray coding ensures only one bit changes per transition, 
    104                                                  //              which prevents incorrect intermediate values during sampling.
    105                                                  // =========================================================================
    106                                                  
    107                                                  // Cross Read Pointer into Write Domain
    108             1                   11314206         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    109                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    110             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    111             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    112                                                      end else begin
    113             1                   11314124                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    114             1                   11314124                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    115                                                      end
    116                                                  end
    117                                              
    118                                                  // Cross Write Pointer into Read Domain
    119             1                   11315380         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    120                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    121             1                         84                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    122             1                         84                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    123                                                      end else begin
    124             1                   11315296                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    125             1                   11315296                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    126                                                      end
    127                                                  end
    128                                              
    129                                                  // =========================================================================
    130                                                  // 4. Status Flag Generation
    131                                                  // Description: Compares Gray-coded pointers to determine FIFO status.
    132                                                  //              - o_empty: True if read pointer equals synchronized write pointer.
    133                                                  //              - o_full : True if write pointer MSBs are inverted relative to 
    134                                                  //                         synchronized read pointer (indicating wrap-around).
    135                                                  // =========================================================================
    136             1                    7858972         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    137             1                    7857784         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    16                                                   parameter WIDTH = 8,
    17                                                   parameter DEPTH = 16
    18                                               )(
    19                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source: D-PHY Clock Domain)
    21                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    22                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,     // D-PHY Byte Clock
    23                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to wr_clk
    24                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,      // Write enable
    25                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,        // Data input from PPI
    26                                                   output wire              o_full,       // FIFO full flag
    27                                               
    28                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination: System Clock Domain)
    30                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,     // Internal System Clock
    32                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to rd_clk
    33                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,      // Read enable from Lane Merger
    34                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,       // Data output to Lane Merger
    35                                                   output wire              o_empty       // FIFO empty flag
    36                                               );

    67                                                       end

    75                                                           end
    76                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin

    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80

    83                                                   // Description: Updates the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.

    86                                                   // =========================================================================

    88                                                       if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin

    90              1                         84                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    91                                                       end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    92              1                    2517162                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;

    94                                                       end
    95                                                   end
    96

    99

    20                                                   // Write Domain Interface (Source: D-PHY Clock Domain)
    21                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    22                                                   input  wire              i_wr_clk,     // D-PHY Byte Clock
    23                                                   input  wire              i_wr_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to wr_clk
    24                                                   input  wire              i_wr_en,      // Write enable
    25                                                   input  wire [WIDTH-1:0]  i_din,        // Data input from PPI
    26                                                   output wire              o_full,       // FIFO full flag
    27                                               
    28                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   // Read Domain Interface (Destination: System Clock Domain)
    30                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   input  wire              i_rd_clk,     // Internal System Clock
    32                                                   input  wire              i_rd_rst_n,   // Reset synchronized to rd_clk
    33                                                   input  wire              i_rd_en,      // Read enable from Lane Merger
    34                                                   output wire [WIDTH-1:0]  o_dout,       // Data output to Lane Merger
    35                                                   output wire              o_empty       // FIFO empty flag
    36                                               );
    37                                               
    38                                                   // Address width calculation
    39                                                   localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);
    40                                               
    41                                                   // Internal Memory and Pointer Registers
    42                                                   reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];
    43                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr, rd_ptr;
    44                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray, rd_ptr_gray; 
    45                                                   
    46                                                   // Cross-Domain Synchronizers (2-Stage / Double-Flop)
    47                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_gray_sync1, wr_ptr_gray_sync2;
    48                                                   reg [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_gray_sync1, rd_ptr_gray_sync2;
    49                                               
    50                                                   // Pointer increments
    51              1                    2517172         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wr_ptr_next = wr_ptr + 1;
    52              1                    2517171         wire [ADDR_WIDTH:0] rd_ptr_next = rd_ptr + 1;
    53                                                   integer j;
    54                                                   // =========================================================================
    55                                                   // 1. Write Domain Logic
    56                                                   // Description: Updates the write pointer and its Gray-coded version.
    57                                                   //              Writes are only performed when 'i_wr_en' is active and FIFO 
    58                                                   //              is not full. Binary pointer is used for memory addressing.
    59                                                   // =========================================================================
    60              1                    5034447         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    61                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    62              1                         82                 wr_ptr <= 0;
    63              1                         82                 wr_ptr_gray <= 0;
    64                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    65              1                    2517162                 wr_ptr <= wr_ptr_next;
    66              1                    2517162                 wr_ptr_gray <= wr_ptr_next ^ (wr_ptr_next >> 1); 
    67                                                       end
    68                                                   end
    69                                               
    70                                                   // Synthesis of the Memory Array (Register-based)
    71              1                    5034447         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    72                                                       if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    73              1                         82                 for (j = 0; j < DEPTH; j = j + 1) begin
    73              2                       1312     
    74              1                       1312                     mem[j] <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    75                                                           end
    76                                                       end else if (i_wr_en && !o_full) begin
    77              1                    2517162                 mem[wr_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]] <= i_din;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // =========================================================================
    82                                                   // 2. Read Domain Logic
    83                                                   // Description: Updates the read pointer and converts it to Gray code.
    84                                                   //              Ensures safe data output only when FIFO is not empty.
    85                                                   //              Gray pointers are used to safely pass the pointer across domains.
    86                                                   // =========================================================================
    87              1                    5064170         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    88                                                       if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    89              1                         84                 rd_ptr <= 0;
    90              1                         84                 rd_ptr_gray <= 0;
    91                                                       end else if (i_rd_en && !o_empty) begin
    92              1                    2517162                 rd_ptr <= rd_ptr_next;
    93              1                    2517162                 rd_ptr_gray <= rd_ptr_next ^ (rd_ptr_next >> 1); 
    94                                                       end
    95                                                   end
    96                                                   
    97                                                   // Data Output Assignment (zeroed if empty to prevent data leakage)
    98              1                    2579939         assign o_dout = (o_empty) ? {WIDTH{1'b0}} : mem[rd_ptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]];
    99                                               
    100                                                  // =========================================================================
    101                                                  // 3. Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) Synchronizers
    102                                                  // Description: Synchronizes pointers between the two asynchronous domains.
    103                                                  //              Gray coding ensures only one bit changes per transition, 
    104                                                  //              which prevents incorrect intermediate values during sampling.
    105                                                  // =========================================================================
    106                                                  
    107                                                  // Cross Read Pointer into Write Domain
    108             1                    7551606         always @(posedge i_wr_clk or negedge i_wr_rst_n) begin
    109                                                      if (!i_wr_rst_n) begin
    110             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    111             1                         82                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    112                                                      end else begin
    113             1                    7551524                 rd_ptr_gray_sync1 <= rd_ptr_gray;
    114             1                    7551524                 rd_ptr_gray_sync2 <= rd_ptr_gray_sync1;
    115                                                      end
    116                                                  end
    117                                              
    118                                                  // Cross Write Pointer into Read Domain
    119             1                    7551612         always @(posedge i_rd_clk or negedge i_rd_rst_n) begin
    120                                                      if (!i_rd_rst_n) begin
    121             1                         84                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= 0;
    122             1                         84                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= 0;
    123                                                      end else begin
    124             1                    7551528                 wr_ptr_gray_sync1 <= wr_ptr_gray;
    125             1                    7551528                 wr_ptr_gray_sync2 <= wr_ptr_gray_sync1;
    126                                                      end
    127                                                  end
    128                                              
    129                                                  // =========================================================================
    130                                                  // 4. Status Flag Generation
    131                                                  // Description: Compares Gray-coded pointers to determine FIFO status.
    132                                                  //              - o_empty: True if read pointer equals synchronized write pointer.
    133                                                  //              - o_full : True if write pointer MSBs are inverted relative to 
    134                                                  //                         synchronized read pointer (indicating wrap-around).
    135                                                  // =========================================================================
    136             1                    5034333         assign o_full  = (wr_ptr_gray == {~rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH:ADDR_WIDTH-1], rd_ptr_gray_sync2[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]});
    137             1                    5034333         assign o_empty = (rd_ptr_gray == wr_ptr_gray_sync2);

    59                                                   // =========================================================================
    60                                                   // CIL Error Handler Logic (100% Glitch-Free CDC)
    61                                                   // Concept: Aggregates multiple asynchronous error sources, captures them 
    62                                                   //          in the source domain, and safely crosses them using a 2-flop sync.
    63                                                   // =========================================================================
    64              1                          2         wire w_rx_error_comb = i_ErrSotHS_0 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_0 | i_ErrControl_0 | i_ErrEsc_0 |
    65                                                                          i_ErrSotHS_1 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_1 | i_ErrControl_1 | i_ErrEsc_1 |
    66                                                                          i_ErrSotHS_2 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_2 | i_ErrControl_2 | i_ErrEsc_2 |
    67                                                                          i_ErrSotHS_3 | i_ErrSotSyncHS_3 | i_ErrControl_3 | i_ErrEsc_3 ;
    68                                               
    69                                                   // Step 1: Register the combined error in the SOURCE domain (RX Clock) to prevent glitches
    70                                                   // Made it STICKY to prevent Data Loss in fast-to-slow crossing
    71                                                   reg r_rx_error_src_domain;
    72              1                        123         always @(posedge i_RxByteClkHS or negedge i_rst_n_rx) begin
    73                                                       if (!i_rst_n_rx) begin
    74              1                         82                 r_rx_error_src_domain <= 1'b0;
    75                                                       end else begin
    76                                                           // Sticky Error: Latch the error high so the System Clock NEVER misses it
    77              1                         41                 r_rx_error_src_domain <= r_rx_error_src_domain | w_rx_error_comb;
    78                                                       end
    79                                                   end
    80                                               
    81                                                   // Step 2: Synchronize the stable signal to the DESTINATION domain (System Clock)
    82                                                   reg [1:0] r_error_sync;
    83              1                        126         always @(posedge i_clk_sys or negedge i_rst_n_sys) begin
    84                                                       if (!i_rst_n_sys) begin
    85              1                         84                 r_error_sync <= 2'b00;
    86                                                       end else begin
    87              1                         42                 r_error_sync <= {r_error_sync[0], r_rx_error_src_domain};
    88                                                       end
    89                                                   end
    90                                                   assign o_rx_error_flag = r_error_sync[1]; // Safely synced to System Clock
    91                                               
    92                                                   // =========================================================================
    93                                                   // RX Data Path Logic
    94                                                   // Architecture: 4 Independent Asynchronous FIFOs to handle Clock Domain 
    95                                                   //               Crossing (CDC) for the payload data from the PHY clock 
    96                                                   //               domain to the core system clock domain.

    141                                                  // =========================================================================
    142                                                  // Synthesis Optimization & Dummy Sink
    143                                                  // Concept: AND-reduction with 1'b0 creates a safe structural sink for 
    144                                                  //          unused interface signals, preventing synthesis tool warnings 
    145                                                  //          and ensuring clean connectivity.
    146                                                  // =========================================================================
    147             1                    6919808         wire w_dummy_sink = &{1'b0, i_RxSyncHS_0, i_RxSyncHS_1, i_RxSyncHS_2, i_RxSyncHS_3,
    148                                                                        w_fifo_full_0, w_fifo_full_1, w_fifo_full_2, w_fifo_full_3,
    149                                                                        i_Shutdown, i_Stopstate_0, i_Stopstate_1, i_Stopstate_2, i_Stopstate_3,
    150                                                                        i_RxUlpmEsc_0, i_RxUlpmEsc_1, i_RxUlpmEsc_2, i_RxUlpmEsc_3};
    151                                              
    152                                                  // The dummy sink is logically OR'ed with o_rx_ready (X | 0 = X)
    153             1                      64364         assign o_rx_ready = (~w_empty_0) | w_dummy_sink; 
    154                                              
    155                                                  // Output assignment & gating
    156             1                    8862479         assign o_lane0_valid = ~w_empty_0 & i_lane0_rd_en;
    157             1                    8860079         assign o_lane1_valid = ~w_empty_1 & i_lane1_rd_en;
    158             1                    8857647         assign o_lane2_valid = ~w_empty_2 & i_lane2_rd_en;
    159             1                    8857647         assign o_lane3_valid = ~w_empty_3 & i_lane3_rd_en;
    160                                              
    161             1                   15085104         assign o_lane0_data = o_lane0_valid ? w_raw_0 : 8'h00;
    162             1                   13207434         assign o_lane1_data = o_lane1_valid ? w_raw_1 : 8'h00;
    163             1                    7570737         assign o_lane2_data = o_lane2_valid ? w_raw_2 : 8'h00;
    164             1                    7571467         assign o_lane3_data = o_lane3_valid ? w_raw_3 : 8'h00;
    165                                              
    166                                                  // =========================================================================
    167                                                  // Packet Active State Machine (Hang Timer)
    168                                                  // Concept: Maintains the active state of the packet for a set duration 
    169                                                  //          (15 cycles) after the FIFO empties. This acts as a tolerance 
    170                                                  //          buffer to prevent premature packet termination during inter-lane 
    171                                                  //          skew or brief interruptions.
    172                                                  // =========================================================================
    173                                                  reg [3:0] rx_hang_timer;
    174                                                  reg r_packet_active;
    175                                                  
    176             1                     188945         always @(posedge i_clk_sys or negedge i_rst_n_sys) begin
    177                                                      if (!i_rst_n_sys) begin
    178             1                         84                 r_packet_active <= 1'b0;
    179             1                         84                 rx_hang_timer   <= 4'd0;
    180                                                      end else begin
    181                                                          if (~w_empty_0) begin 
    182             1                      64318                     r_packet_active <= 1'b1;
    183             1                      64318                     rx_hang_timer   <= 4'd15; 
    184                                                          end else if (rx_hang_timer > 0) begin
    185             1                     116497                     rx_hang_timer   <= rx_hang_timer - 1;
    186                                                          end else begin
    187             1                       8046                     r_packet_active <= 1'b0;

    14                                               module mipi_descrambler #(
    15                                                   parameter [15:0] LANE_SEED = 16'h0810 
    16                                               )(
    17                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    19                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    21                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    22                                                   
    23                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                                   // Control & Input Data Stream
    25                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    26                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active,  // Resets LFSR to SEED when de-asserted
    27                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_scrambled_data, 
    28                                                   input  wire        i_lane_vld,       // Data valid for the current lane
    29                                                   
    30                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   // Descrambled Data Outputs

    34                                                   output wire        o_lane_vld_out
    35                                               );
    36                                               
    37                                                   // LFSR State Register
    38                                                   reg  [15:0] r_lfsr_state;
    39                                                   
    40                                                   // Intermediate wires for Parallel Logic evaluation
    41                                                   wire [7:0]  w_key_stream;
    42                                                   wire [7:0]  w_fb;
    43                                                   wire [15:0] w_lfsr_next;
    44                                               
    45                                                   // =========================================================================
    46                                                   // 1. Hardware Unrolling (Parallel Feedback Evaluation)
    47                                                   // Description: Calculates 8 bits of LFSR feedback in a single cycle using 
    48                                                   //              Combinational Logic. This eliminates the need for 'for-loops' 
    49                                                   //              and avoids multiple driver issues during synthesis.
    50                                                   // Polynomial: Evaluates the specific MIPI-standard scrambling taps.
    51                                                   // =========================================================================
    52              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[0] = r_lfsr_state[0] ^ r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5];
    53              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[1] = r_lfsr_state[1] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6];
    54              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[2] = r_lfsr_state[2] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7];
    55              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[3] = r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8];
    56              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[4] = r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9];
    57              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[5] = r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10];
    58              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[6] = r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11];
    59              1                    6283357         assign w_fb[7] = r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11] ^ r_lfsr_state[12];
    60                                               
    61                                                   // The key-stream is the lowest 8 bits of the current state
    62              1                    6283357         assign w_key_stream = r_lfsr_state[7:0];
    63                                               
    64                                                   // Construct the next 16-bit state by shifting and inserting feedback bits
    65              1                   12530665         assign w_lfsr_next  = {w_fb[7], w_fb[6], w_fb[5], w_fb[4], w_fb[3], w_fb[2], w_fb[1], w_fb[0], r_lfsr_state[15:8]};
    66                                               
    67                                                   // =========================================================================
    68                                                   // 2. LFSR State Controller
    69                                                   // Description: Manages initialization and state transitions.
    70                                                   //              - Reset or Idle: Re-initializes state to the 'LANE_SEED'.
    71                                                   //              - Active Reception: Advances the LFSR state per valid byte.
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73              1                   10690544         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    74              1                         84             if (!i_rst_n)                 r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    75              1                       8046             else if (!i_packet_active)    r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    76              1                    6279354             else if (i_lane_vld)          r_lfsr_state <= w_lfsr_next;
    77                                                   end
    78                                               
    79                                                   // =========================================================================
    80                                                   // 3. Descrambling Operation (XOR)
    81                                                   // Description: Recovers the original data by XORing the scrambled input 
    82                                                   //              with the generated key-stream. Output is gated by 'i_lane_vld'.
    83                                                   // =========================================================================
    84              1                   30032130         assign o_descrambled_data = i_lane_vld ? (i_scrambled_data ^ w_key_stream) : 8'h00;

================================Statement Details================================

Statement Coverage for instance /\csi_tb_top /dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_desc2 --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File mipi_descrambler.v
    14                                               module mipi_descrambler #(
    15                                                   parameter [15:0] LANE_SEED = 16'h0810 
    16                                               )(
    17                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    19                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    21                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    22                                                   
    23                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                                   // Control & Input Data Stream
    25                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    26                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active,  // Resets LFSR to SEED when de-asserted
    27                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_scrambled_data, 
    28                                                   input  wire        i_lane_vld,       // Data valid for the current lane
    29                                                   
    30                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   // Descrambled Data Outputs
    32                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    33                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_descrambled_data,
    34                                                   output wire        o_lane_vld_out
    35                                               );
    36                                               
    37                                                   // LFSR State Register
    38                                                   reg  [15:0] r_lfsr_state;
    39                                                   
    40                                                   // Intermediate wires for Parallel Logic evaluation
    41                                                   wire [7:0]  w_key_stream;
    42                                                   wire [7:0]  w_fb;
    43                                                   wire [15:0] w_lfsr_next;
    44                                               
    45                                                   // =========================================================================
    46                                                   // 1. Hardware Unrolling (Parallel Feedback Evaluation)
    47                                                   // Description: Calculates 8 bits of LFSR feedback in a single cycle using 
    48                                                   //              Combinational Logic. This eliminates the need for 'for-loops' 
    49                                                   //              and avoids multiple driver issues during synthesis.
    50                                                   // Polynomial: Evaluates the specific MIPI-standard scrambling taps.
    51                                                   // =========================================================================
    52              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[0] = r_lfsr_state[0] ^ r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5];
    53              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[1] = r_lfsr_state[1] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6];
    54              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[2] = r_lfsr_state[2] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7];
    55              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[3] = r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8];
    56              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[4] = r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9];
    57              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[5] = r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10];
    58              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[6] = r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11];
    59              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[7] = r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11] ^ r_lfsr_state[12];
    60                                               
    61                                                   // The key-stream is the lowest 8 bits of the current state
    62              1                    2518765         assign w_key_stream = r_lfsr_state[7:0];
    63                                               
    64                                                   // Construct the next 16-bit state by shifting and inserting feedback bits
    65              1                    5034311         assign w_lfsr_next  = {w_fb[7], w_fb[6], w_fb[5], w_fb[4], w_fb[3], w_fb[2], w_fb[1], w_fb[0], r_lfsr_state[15:8]};
    66                                               
    67                                                   // =========================================================================
    68                                                   // 2. LFSR State Controller
    69                                                   // Description: Manages initialization and state transitions.
    70                                                   //              - Reset or Idle: Re-initializes state to the 'LANE_SEED'.
    71                                                   //              - Active Reception: Advances the LFSR state per valid byte.
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73              1                    5044056         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    74              1                         84             if (!i_rst_n)                 r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    75              1                       5646             else if (!i_packet_active)    r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    76              1                    2517162             else if (i_lane_vld)          r_lfsr_state <= w_lfsr_next;
    77                                                   end
    78                                               
    79                                                   // =========================================================================
    80                                                   // 3. Descrambling Operation (XOR)
    81                                                   // Description: Recovers the original data by XORing the scrambled input 
    82                                                   //              with the generated key-stream. Output is gated by 'i_lane_vld'.
    83                                                   // =========================================================================
    84              1                   15041712         assign o_descrambled_data = i_lane_vld ? (i_scrambled_data ^ w_key_stream) : 8'h00;

Statement Coverage for instance /\csi_tb_top /dut/u_csi2_rx_top/u_desc3 --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File mipi_descrambler.v
    14                                               module mipi_descrambler #(
    15                                                   parameter [15:0] LANE_SEED = 16'h0810 
    16                                               )(
    17                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    19                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    20                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    21                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    22                                                   
    23                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24                                                   // Control & Input Data Stream
    25                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    26                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active,  // Resets LFSR to SEED when de-asserted
    27                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_scrambled_data, 
    28                                                   input  wire        i_lane_vld,       // Data valid for the current lane
    29                                                   
    30                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   // Descrambled Data Outputs
    32                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    33                                                   output wire [7:0]  o_descrambled_data,
    34                                                   output wire        o_lane_vld_out
    35                                               );
    36                                               
    37                                                   // LFSR State Register
    38                                                   reg  [15:0] r_lfsr_state;
    39                                                   
    40                                                   // Intermediate wires for Parallel Logic evaluation
    41                                                   wire [7:0]  w_key_stream;
    42                                                   wire [7:0]  w_fb;
    43                                                   wire [15:0] w_lfsr_next;
    44                                               
    45                                                   // =========================================================================
    46                                                   // 1. Hardware Unrolling (Parallel Feedback Evaluation)
    47                                                   // Description: Calculates 8 bits of LFSR feedback in a single cycle using 
    48                                                   //              Combinational Logic. This eliminates the need for 'for-loops' 
    49                                                   //              and avoids multiple driver issues during synthesis.
    50                                                   // Polynomial: Evaluates the specific MIPI-standard scrambling taps.
    51                                                   // =========================================================================
    52              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[0] = r_lfsr_state[0] ^ r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5];
    53              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[1] = r_lfsr_state[1] ^ r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6];
    54              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[2] = r_lfsr_state[2] ^ r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7];
    55              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[3] = r_lfsr_state[3] ^ r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8];
    56              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[4] = r_lfsr_state[4] ^ r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9];
    57              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[5] = r_lfsr_state[5] ^ r_lfsr_state[8] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10];
    58              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[6] = r_lfsr_state[6] ^ r_lfsr_state[9] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11];
    59              1                    2518765         assign w_fb[7] = r_lfsr_state[7] ^ r_lfsr_state[10] ^ r_lfsr_state[11] ^ r_lfsr_state[12];
    60                                               
    61                                                   // The key-stream is the lowest 8 bits of the current state
    62              1                    2518765         assign w_key_stream = r_lfsr_state[7:0];
    63                                               
    64                                                   // Construct the next 16-bit state by shifting and inserting feedback bits
    65              1                    5028295         assign w_lfsr_next  = {w_fb[7], w_fb[6], w_fb[5], w_fb[4], w_fb[3], w_fb[2], w_fb[1], w_fb[0], r_lfsr_state[15:8]};
    66                                               
    67                                                   // =========================================================================
    68                                                   // 2. LFSR State Controller
    69                                                   // Description: Manages initialization and state transitions.
    70                                                   //              - Reset or Idle: Re-initializes state to the 'LANE_SEED'.
    71                                                   //              - Active Reception: Advances the LFSR state per valid byte.
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73              1                    5044056         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    74              1                         84             if (!i_rst_n)                 r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    75              1                       5646             else if (!i_packet_active)    r_lfsr_state <= LANE_SEED;
    76              1                    2517162             else if (i_lane_vld)          r_lfsr_state <= w_lfsr_next;
    77                                                   end
    78                                               
    79                                                   // =========================================================================
    80                                                   // 3. Descrambling Operation (XOR)
    81                                                   // Description: Recovers the original data by XORing the scrambled input 
    82                                                   //              with the generated key-stream. Output is gated by 'i_lane_vld'.
    83                                                   // =========================================================================
    84              1                   15036473         assign o_descrambled_data = i_lane_vld ? (i_scrambled_data ^ w_key_stream) : 8'h00;

  File lane_merger.v
    13                                               module lane_merger (
    14                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15                                                   // System Clock & Reset
    16                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    17                                                   input  wire        i_clk,
    18                                                   input  wire        i_rst_n,
    19                                                   
    20                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21                                                   // Configuration & Control Signals
    22                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    23                                                   input  wire [2:0]  i_active_lanes, 
    24                                                   input  wire        i_packet_active, 
    25                                                   input  wire        i_rx_ready,     
    26                                               
    27                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28                                                   // Parallel Data Inputs (From PPI RX FIFOs)
    29                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    30                                                   input  wire [7:0]  i_lane0_data, i_lane1_data, i_lane2_data, i_lane3_data,
    31                                                   input  wire        i_lane0_vld,  i_lane1_vld,  i_lane2_vld,  i_lane3_vld, 
    32                                               
    33                                                   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34                                                   // Serial Data Outputs & FIFO Pull Control
    35                                                   // ---------------------------------------------------------
    36                                                   output wire        o_pull_data,
    37                                                   output reg  [7:0]  o_merged_data,
    38                                                   output reg         o_merged_valid
    39                                               );
    40                                               
    41                                                   // Internal Buffers & FSM Registers
    42                                                   reg [7:0] r_buf1, r_buf2, r_buf3;
    43                                                   reg [3:1] r_vld_buf; 
    44                                                   reg [1:0] r_count;
    45                                                   reg [1:0] next_count; 
    46                                                   
    47                                                   reg [2:0] startup_delay;
    48                                                   reg       r_is_active;
    49                                               
    50                                                   // =========================================================================
    51                                                   // 1. Startup Delay & Active State Controller
    52                                                   // Description: Introduces a brief initialization delay (3 cycles) upon 
    53                                                   //              receiving 'i_rx_ready' before starting to pull data. This 
    54                                                   //              ensures data alignment and prevents premature reads.
    55                                                   // =========================================================================
    56              1                   13863401         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin
    57                                                       if (!i_rst_n) begin
    58              1                         84                 startup_delay <= 3'd3;
    59              1                         84                 r_is_active   <= 1'b0;
    60                                                       end else if (!i_packet_active && (r_count == 2'd0) && !o_pull_data) begin
    61              1                      12049                 startup_delay <= 3'd3;
    62              1                      12049                 r_is_active   <= 1'b0;
    63                                                       end else if (i_rx_ready && !r_is_active) begin
    64              1                      12009                 if (startup_delay > 0) startup_delay <= startup_delay - 1;
    65              1                       4003                 else                   r_is_active <= 1'b1;
    66                                                       end
    67                                                   end
    68                                               
    69                                                   // Pull condition: Only assert read enable when active, in FSM state 0, and RX is ready
    70              1                   13843263         assign o_pull_data = r_is_active && (r_count == 2'd0) && i_rx_ready;
    71                                                   
    72                                                   // =========================================================================
    73                                                   // 2. Output Multiplexer FSM (Next State Logic)
    74                                                   // Description: Determines the sequence of outputting lanes based on the 
    75                                                   //              active lane configuration (1, 2, or 4 lanes). Loops back 
    76                                                   //              to state 0 to fetch the next parallel word.
    77                                                   // =========================================================================
    78              1                   13835294         always @(*) begin
    79              1                   13835294             next_count = r_count;
    80                                                       case (r_count)
    81              1                    4397858                 2'd0: if (o_pull_data && i_active_lanes > 1) next_count = 2'd1;
    82              1                    2517161                 2'd1: if (i_active_lanes > 2) next_count = 2'd2; else next_count = 2'd0;
    82              2                    1880696

    87                                                   end
    88                                               
    89                                                   // =========================================================================
    90                                                   // 3. Datapath & Pipelined Buffering
    91                                                   // Description: 
    92                                                   //   - State 0: Outputs Lane 0 directly and concurrently buffers Lanes 1/2/3.
    93                                                   //   - State 1/2/3: Streams the buffered data sequentially out to the MAC.
    94                                                   //   This ensures zero dead-cycles when converting parallel to serial.
    95                                                   // =========================================================================
    96              1                   15727702         always @(posedge i_clk or negedge i_rst_n) begin

    101             2                         84     
    101             3                         84     
    102             1                         84                 r_vld_buf <= 3'b000;
    103                                                      end else if (!i_packet_active && (r_count == 2'd0) && !o_pull_data) begin
    104             1                       4044                 r_count        <= 2'd0;
    105             1                       4044                 o_merged_data  <= 8'h00; 
    106             1                       4044                 o_merged_valid <= 1'b0;
    107                                                      end else begin
    108             1                   15723574                 r_count <= next_count;
    109                                                          case (r_count)
    110                                                              2'd0: begin
    111                                                                  if (o_pull_data) begin
    112                                                                      // Directly output Lane 0
    113             1                    6279354                             o_merged_data  <= i_lane0_vld ? i_lane0_data : 8'h00;
    114             1                    6279354                             o_merged_valid <= i_lane0_vld; 
    115
